intro: Turn W-9 paperwork into clean PDFs for vendor onboarding, tax prep, and compliance workflows where names, addresses, and tax details must stay readable.
heroBullets: Preserve business names, addresses, and tax-identification fields
Create cleaner vendor-onboarding packets from mobile
Keep signed tax forms ready for finance, compliance, or resend requests
keyProblems: W-9 forms are detail-heavy and often signed under deadline pressure
Vendors and finance teams need a cleaner copy than an uneven phone photo
The same form often needs to be resent later when onboarding stalls
workflowSteps: Capture the full form with edges and signatures visible
Enhance the scan so tax fields and addresses stay legible
Export one polished PDF for onboarding, filing, or archive

uniqueContentBlocks.heading: Support vendor onboarding
uniqueContentBlocks.body: W-9 intent is tied to a concrete business workflow. The page should explain how ScanDocPro helps people create a cleaner onboarding file without going back to a desk scanner.

uniqueContentBlocks.heading: Keep sensitive fields readable
uniqueContentBlocks.body: A W-9 page should focus on preserving names, addresses, and tax-related fields because that is what makes the document useful after it is scanned.

uniqueContentBlocks.heading: Reduce resend friction
uniqueContentBlocks.body: These forms get requested again. Building a polished digital copy once is more valuable than taking a one-off snapshot every time someone asks.

faq.question: Why add a W-9 page to the document family?
faq.answer: Because vendor and tax onboarding searches are specific, commercially relevant, and aligned with ScanDocPro's strengths in readability and clean mobile export.

faq.question: What should the page emphasize most?
faq.answer: Readable tax fields, visible signatures, and a polished PDF that is ready for finance or compliance workflows.

name: Proof of Address
intro: Build clean proof-of-address PDFs for tenant screening, onboarding, account setup, and compliance requests that need readable names, addresses, and dates.
heroBullets: Package bills, statements, and ID-supporting pages into one proof set
Keep addresses, names, and issue dates readable
Send a cleaner verification packet without rebuilding it from scratch
keyProblems: Proof-of-address requests often arrive with short deadlines
The workflow usually requires more than one supporting document
A low-quality scan creates unnecessary back-and-forth with the requester
workflowSteps: Collect the bill, statement, or supporting pages the requester accepts
Batch scan them so names, addresses, and dates stay easy to review
Export one organized PDF for screening, onboarding, or compliance

uniqueContentBlocks.heading: Build a verification packet
uniqueContentBlocks.body: This page should frame proof-of-address as a small packet-building job, not a single random scan. That makes ScanDocPro's batch and cleanup story more useful.

uniqueContentBlocks.heading: Prioritize the fields people check
uniqueContentBlocks.body: Names, addresses, and recent dates are the real point of the workflow. The page should keep attention on whether those details are clear enough to approve quickly.

uniqueContentBlocks.heading: Reduce request-round trips
uniqueContentBlocks.body: A strong proof-of-address page should promise fewer resend cycles because the first PDF is already neat, readable, and complete.

faq.question: Why separate proof-of-address from utility-bill scanning?
faq.answer: Because the search intent is broader. People are usually trying to satisfy a verification request and may need several supporting documents together.

faq.question: What should this page help users do?
faq.answer: It should help them build one clean PDF packet with readable names, addresses, and dates that a landlord, lender, school, or office can review easily.
